chunks.text: Pieter Abbeel is a Professor at UC Berkeley EECS with office in 746 Sutardja Dai Hall. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR). He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2008 and his M.S. in Electrical Engineering from KU Leuven, Belgium in 2000. Office hours are arranged via email.
Ahmed Alaa is a Below The Line Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley EECS with email amalaa@berkeley.edu. He earned his Ph.D. in EECS from UCLA in 2020.

Elad Alon is an Adjunct Professor at UC Berkeley EECS with office in 519 Cory Hall. His research interests include Integrated Circuits (INC), Design, Modeling and Analysis (DMA), Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), and Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC). He earned his Ph.D., M.S., and B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University in 2006, 2002, and 2001 respectively. Office hours are Tu. 2:30-3:30pm in 519 Cory.

Venkat Anantharam is a Professor at UC Berkeley EECS with office in 271 Cory Hall. His research interests include Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), Artificial Intelligence (AI), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Security (SEC), and Signal Processing (SP). He earned his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from UC Berkeley in 1986, with additional degrees in Mathematics from UC Berkeley. Office hours vary by week, and assistants include Kim Kail. Teaching schedule includes EE 223 and EE 227BT in Spring 2026.

Gopala Krishna Anumanchipalli is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley EECS with office in 490A Cory Hall. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Signal Processing (SP), and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). He earned his Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Instituto Superior Técnico, Lisbon, Portugal in 2013, and another Ph.D. in Language and Information Technologies from Carnegie Mellon University in 2013. Office hours are 2:30-430PM in 490A Cory. Teaching schedule includes EE 123 in Spring 2026.

Mekhail Anwar is an Adjunct Professor at UC Berkeley EECS with email mekhail@berkeley.edu. His research interests include Integrated Circuits (INC), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), and Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems (MEMS).

Murat Arcak is a Professor at UC Berkeley EECS with office in 569 Cory Hall. His research interests include Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Cyber-Physical Systems and Design Automation (CPSDA), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), and Power and Energy (ENE). He earned his Ph.D. and M.S. in Electrical Engineering from UC Santa Barbara in 2000 and 1997, and his B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Bogazici University, Istanbul, Turkey in 1996.

Ana Claudia Arias is a Professor and EE Division Chair at UC Berkeley EECS with offices in 501 Cory Hall and 225 Cory Hall. Her research interests include Physical Electronics (PHY) and Power and Energy (ENE). She earned her Ph.D. in Physics from University of Cambridge, UK in 2001, and her M.S. and B.S. in Physics from Federal University of Paraná in Curitiba, Brazil in 1997 and 1995. Office hours are by appointment in 508 Cory, with assistant Alex Sandoval.

Krste Asanović is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley EECS with office in 579B Soda Hall. His research interests include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), Integrated Circuits (INC), Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), and Design, Modeling and Analysis (DMA). He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 1998 and his B.A. in Electrical and Information Sciences from University of Cambridge, UK in 1987. Office hours are by email appointment, with assistants Tammy Johnson and Ria Melendres Briggs.

David Attwood is a Professor in Residence Emeritus at UC Berkeley EECS with email no_email@eecs.berkeley.edu. He earned his Ph.D. in Applied Physics from New York University in 1972. Office hours are by e-mail appointment, meeting at Yali's Stanley Hall.
